feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Patients are dying in A&E corridors - but I've seen how things could be different | Sophie
entry.content: When I started nursing at 21 we were able to deliver timely, good care. That has become nearly impossibleSophie (not her real name) is a member of the Royal College of Nursing and a senior A&E nurse in a hospital in the south of EnglandI began my career as an A&E nurse in 2010, when I was 21. It was a completely different world. If a patient needed immediate attention, there was easily the capacity for two nurses to look after them straight away. The NHS target of seeing patients within a four-hour window wasn’t something we gave much thought to, as it was pretty much a given that a patient would be admitted, transferred or discharged within that time. I don’t ever recall seeing a patient and feeling awful about how long they had waited.It’s amazing to think how common it used to be for emergency departments to be almost empty at times in the evenings. As well as being much needed respite from the demands of the job, it was also a valuable time to learn from more senior colleagues. Nurses with decades of experience would take new recruits under their wings and help us practise our skills. That time is when I learned to plaster limbs and dress wounds. I wish I could do the same for my junior colleagues now. We used to be able to give timely, good care – now it has become near impossible.Sophie (not her real name) is a member of the Royal College of Nursing and a senior A&E nurse in a hospital in the south of EnglandDo you have an opinion on the issues raised in this article? If you would like to submit a response of up to 300 words by email to be considered for publication in our letters section, please click here. Continue reading...

entry.title: Nearly 3,000 NHS patients a day receiving corridor care in England, figures show
entry.content: Published for the first time, the data recorded 2,241 daily cases of A&E corridor care, with 699 patients also treated in other inappropriate settingsAlmost 3,000 patients a day in England are receiving care in hospital corridors due to an unavailability of beds in A&E units across the country, according to official figures.Corridor care occurs when a patient receives treatment in a setting that is clinically inappropriate and is deemed to be undignified and unsafe. Continue reading...

entry.title: Stranger than Heaven hands-on: Harder than Yakuza?
entry.content: Set across mid-twentieth century Japan, RGG's latest has an entirely new fighting system.

entry.title: Toronto police officer shot while investigating US consulate attack
entry.content: Officer shot before 6am on Thursday while searching an apartment allegedly linked to March shooting in TorontoA Toronto police officer has been shot as police raided an apartment allegedly linked to the March attack on the US consulate.Toronto police confirmed an officer was shot before 6am while conducting a search warrant in the north-west of the city, and has been rushed to hospital. Continue reading...

entry.title: I'm glad Apple isn't hyping up agentic AI (yet)
entry.content: Apple is more focused on delivering usable features with Siri AI, instead of hyping up agentic AI. That's a good thing.

entry.title: Artemis III's life support: How a spacesuit keeps astronauts alive on the moon
entry.content: The spaceman wears Prada.

entry.title: Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis is a vivid, high-pace remake of a classic
entry.content: Lara get your gun(s).

entry.title: Former Fitbit executive unveils new necklace that tracks UV exposure
entry.content: The Gem necklace can track your UV exposure.

entry.title: Cuba hopes for World Cup respite from US sabre-rattling – but prepares for the worst
entry.content: With some matches being held in nearby Miami, a Cuban response to US military action could mar the tournamentAs Cuba crumbles under a nearly five-month-long US oil blockade, many on the island hope that the World Cup might save the island from US attack – or at least offer a respite until the competition ends on 19 July.“The beginning of the World Cup will make it more difficult for the United States to carry out a military action in Cuba,” said Carlos Alzugaray, Cuba’s former ambassador to the EU. “Cuba is very close to the US, and can hit many targets inside the US, especially in south Florida, with drones or other weapons.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Engadget's favorite Game Boy Advance games
entry.content: Join us for a trip down memory lane on the 25th anniversary of the GBA's US release.

entry.title: Too sick to work, but can they prove it? New Medicaid rule worries patients
entry.content: Blood cancer survivor DeAnna Brandon worries about new Medicaid work requirements affecting her health coverage

entry.title: Storms knock out power in the Midwest and disrupt Chicago flights
entry.content: Damaging storms are sweeping through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of customers

entry.title: Bluesky will launch Reddit-style communities this year
entry.content: Bluesky is launching a communities feature this year, according to its head of product.

entry.title: How Wild the Market’s Bet on AI Really Is
entry.content: Plus, an artificial intelligence price war may be brewing

entry.title: Nearly 118 million people were displaced by conflict, persecution last year, UN says
entry.content: The U.N. refugee agency reports that forced displacement due to conflict or persecution fell in 2025 for the first time in a decade

entry.title: Two children die from measles as England data shows 100 new infections
entry.content: London, the east of England and the West Midlands have highest number of cases, as UKHSA urges families to get children vaccinatedTwo children in England have died from measles, health officials say, as data shows more than 100 new reported cases in the last fortnight.The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said on Thursday that two children had died this year, one from “acute measles” and the other from the “late effects of measles”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Deezer now helps users find AI music on other streaming platforms
entry.content: Deezer will now help you find AI slop in your music playlists even if you're on another platform.

entry.title: Those tedious errands, tasks and chores that AI wants to replace? They help keep you fit | Manoush Zomorodi and Keith Diaz
entry.content: There’s a downside to too much convenience: it harms our bodiesThere is a seductive fantasy being floated by AI executives that all the efficiency their products will bring us will lead to humans finally returning to their essential, best selves. Picture it: when this day arrives, we’ll spring from our chairs, push aside our keyboards and, supposedly, do all things we’ve been meaning to do: hike, cook and finally take a pilates class.It’s true – AI has already taken some workday drudgery, such as reading and writing contracts, presentations and quarterly reports, off some people’s plates. Within a few years, we’re told, a team of invisible digital assistants will take over mundane domestic chores too: making medical appointments, renewing our car insurance and planning. The vision is enticing: finally, the moment when we can stop switching-switching-switching between screens and devices, put our health first and flourish. Unfortunately, if the history of innovation teaches us anything, it’s that labor-saving technology has rarely, if ever, triggered healthier habits. Drive-throughs and microwaves did not lead to more time spent walking in nature. When escalators replaced stairs, email took over from walking over to talk to a colleague, and wandering through the video store was swapped out for streaming from the couch, few of us considered how these tiny conveniences would chip away at our physical health, year after more efficient year. A task that took almost no effort used to be described with the saying: “You hardly need to lift a finger.” Now, we literally lift a finger and – tap – the chore is done. Continue reading...

entry.title: Brazilian woman who is 38 accused of years of ‘elaborate fraud schemes’ by posing as a child
entry.content: Police who arrested Amanda Maria Souza de Oliveira say she admitted that she had lied to obtain financial supportA 38-year-old woman has been arrested in Brazil accused of pretending to be a 12-year-old girl to deceive a couple who took her into their home for more than a year.Amanda Maria Souza de Oliveira was charged in the southern state of Santa Catarina with fraud and false identity offences. Continue reading...

entry.title: World Cup Group L standings 2026: Updated results, table and fixtures for England, Croatia, Ghana, Panama
entry.content: Here's a complete guide to Group L's teams, match schedule and fixtures for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: Framework delays Laptop 13 Pro shipments by a month
entry.content: Framework is pushing back shipments of its new Laptop 13 Pro to address issues with its haptic touchpad and display found in the run-up to mass production.

entry.title: Meta’s Subscription Push Exposes Its Weak Hand in AI
entry.content: Charging users is the latest idea to expand beyond ads—a pickle Meta isn’t likely to get out of soon.

entry.title: Rent Is So High, New Yorkers Are Living With Nuns
entry.content: Convent boarding houses are affordable and clean, but there are rules: curfews, chores and sometimes nuns who vet boyfriends.

entry.title: World Cup group tiebreakers 2026: Explaining what happens if teams are tied on points, goal differential, and fair play
entry.content: Here's how the group-stage tiebreaker works to determine which teams move on to the knockouts.

entry.title: Anthropic backtracks on policy that 'sabotaged' researchers' work
entry.content: Anthropic is walking back a hidden policy that researchers say sabotaged their work.

entry.title: How to watch World Cup 2026: TV channels, live streams and fixture schedule for FIFA tournament
entry.content: The FIFA World Cup is considered the most illustrious competition on the global football calendar.

entry.title: Where to watch Mexico World Cup opening ceremony: Live stream, TV channel and start time for show featuring Shakira
entry.content: Who is performing at Mexico World Cup opening ceremony and what times does it start? Before the opening match of the tournament between Mexico and South Africa, we're set to be treated to some musical performances from the biggest stars.

entry.title: World Cup Group K standings 2026: Updated results, table and fixtures for Portugal, Colombia, Uzbekistan, DR Congo
entry.content: Here's a complete guide to Group K's teams, match schedule and fixtures for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: Where to watch South Korea vs. Czechia live stream, TV channel, start time for World Cup Group A match
entry.content: South Korea vs. Czechia kickoff time: Here’s how to watch the World Cup match on TV and live stream. The Sporting News takes you through all the information you need to follow the game.

entry.title: Who is Tyla? Meet the South Africa national anthem singer for 2026 World Cup opening ceremony
entry.content: Tyla has gained international fame for her recent pop hits. Now she takes her talent to the international stage to sing South Africa's national anthem at the World Cup.

entry.title: Who is Alejandro Fernandez? Meet the Mexican national anthem singer for 2026 World Cup opening ceremony
entry.content: Alejandro Fernandez is a staple of the Mexican music scene. Now, he takes his talents to the international stage at this year's World Cup.

entry.title: FDA approves new sunscreen ingredient
entry.content: The FDA approved a new sunscreen ingredient for the U.S. Bemotrizinol has long been used in Europe and Asia. The chemical compound protects against UVA and UVB rays.

entry.title: White House's response to hantavirus and Ebola is at odds with COVID criticisms
entry.content: The Trump administration has imposed tough restrictions in response to hantavirus and Ebola, including quarantine orders and travel bans. That's despite past criticism of many COVID measures.

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Investors Shrug Off Iran Tensions, Oracle Earnings
entry.content: Stock futures gain with oil down slightly

entry.title: YouTube expands direct messaging to the US
entry.content: YouTube is testing DMs in its mobile app, and US is now part of the experiment.

entry.title: Judge considers arguments in challenge to New Mexico's universal childcare program
entry.content: It is up to a New Mexico judge to settle a challenge over the legality of New Mexico’s fledgling universal childcare program, an ambitious and closely watched effort to eliminate daycare costs for all working families

entry.title: FISA spy program at risk over Trump's pick of Pulte for director of national intelligence
entry.content: A rare lapse in a law that allows the U.S. to gather intelligence abroad has grown more likely

entry.title: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build a new US Consulate in Milan
entry.content: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build a new US Consulate in Milan

entry.title: The Knicks pulled off the largest comeback in NBA Finals history. Here’s how it happened.
entry.content: The New York Knicks overcame a 29-point deficit against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 to take a 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals. Here’s how it happened.

entry.title: Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs" if needed; U.S. announces more strikes
entry.content: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as U.S. forces struck Iran again Wednesday after President Trump pledged Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

entry.title: Here's who will face off in California's new House districts as Dems seek edge
entry.content: California's Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom spearheaded a redistricting initiative that was intended to make up to five more districts more friendly to Democrats. Voters may have different ideas.

entry.title: Is China's Xi worried that North Korea wants "to confront the U.S."?
entry.content: A veteran North Korea analyst says Xi Jinping's visit to North Korea was about keeping tabs on an emboldened Kim Jong Un who wants "to confront the U.S."

entry.title: U.S. launches more strikes on Iranian targets after Trump promises to hit Iran "hard"
entry.content: The U.S. military launched an additional round of strikes on targets within Iran early Thursday morning local time, hours after President Trump vowed to hit Iran "hard."

entry.title: Tempo's Brittney Sykes is doing something no one else in WNBA has done this season
entry.content: Sykes continues to get buckets for the expansion team in Toronto.

entry.title: Spurs vs. Knicks live score, updates, highlights from 2026 NBA Finals Game 4
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights from Spurs vs. Knicks in Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals. Follow for complete results from Madison Square Garden.

entry.title: Why Victor Wembanyama is at risk of suspension in NBA Finals after latest flagrant foul
entry.content: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama will have to be careful after his latest flagrant foul.

entry.title: Seth Lugo injury update: Royals pitcher takes scary, 106.6 MPH line drive off head hit by Brandon Nimmo
entry.content: This was a tough one to watch as Lugo had to exit the game.

entry.title: Belfast protests erupt again after stabbing as police deploy water cannons
entry.content: Police have used water cannons on protesters in Northern Ireland after violence erupted for a second night over a stabbing in Belfast.

entry.title: Spurs vs Knicks box score: Full stats from NBA Finals Game 4
entry.content: Spurs look to even the series on the road.

entry.title: Bryce Eldridge's grand slam caps Giants' historical rally over Nationals
entry.content: San Francisco became the first team in MLB history to win on a walk-off slam after trailing by eight runs in the eighth inning.

entry.title: Most 3-pointers in NBA Finals game: How Spurs' Game 4 explosion vs. Knicks compares to all-time record
entry.content: The Spurs took a run at an NBA record with their 3-point success in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

entry.title: Why Taylor Swift wore a 'Stevie Knicks' shirt to Game 4 of the NBA Finals
entry.content: Quite the comical look for the pop star.

entry.title: Inside Trump Mobile
entry.content: A scanner, microscope and an old-fashioned teardown of Trump Mobile’s T1 phone showed that it was almost identical to the HTC U24 Pro. NBC News' Brian Cheung explains.

entry.title: Alabama death row prisoner fighting execution speaks out amid legal battle
entry.content: In an phone interview from Alabama's death row, Jeffery Lee said he was "emotional" when he heard the news that a federal judge blocked his execution - but his fate still hangs in the balance. NBC News' Laura Jarrett reports.

entry.title: They came for the World Cup. They fell in love with Buc-ee’s and ranch dip.
entry.content: International tourists traveling through the U.S. ahead of FIFA’s festivities have been documenting their American experiences on social media.

entry.title: Far-right influencer arrested in Texas on charge of making terroristic threats
entry.content: Jake Lang, a far-right influencer who was charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol insurrection, was arrested in Dallas on a state charge of making terroristic threats.

entry.title: Survey finds 51% of Americans feel financially conflicted
entry.content: A new poll from Edward Jones and Gallup of more than 5,000 adults found only 16% felt financially "fulfilled." More than half said they feel financially "conflicted," experiencing both stability and uncertainty. 32% were financially stressed. Washington Post columnist Michelle Singletary joins "The Daily Report" to discuss her article about the poll.

entry.title: Judge pauses Trump administration efforts to add conditions to SNAP benefits
entry.content: A federal judge has halted an effort by the Trump administration to force states to comply with conditions tied to SNAP food benefits. Twenty Democratic states sued over requirements surrounding gender ideology, immigration and fair opportunities in sports for women and girls. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson answers constitutional questions about the case.

entry.title: Doctor explains why bemotrizinol, a sunscreen ingredient, took so long to be authorized
entry.content: The Food and Drug Administration approved the chemical compound bemotrizinol in sunscreens sold in the U.S. It's the first time in over 25 years that the FDA has approved a new sunscreen ingredient. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

entry.title: Ret. Army Maj. Mike Lyons on new U.S. strikes against Iran: "It's kind of about time"
entry.content: The U.S. launched strikes against Iran on Wednesday night following the downing of an Apache helicopter earlier in the week. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson has the latest. Then, Ret. Army Maj. Mike Lyons joins with analysis.

entry.title: In a tough economy, many U.S. teachers forced to work second jobs
entry.content: According to a recent survey, 71% of U.S. public school teachers said they work at least one second job.

entry.title: The House Just Passed a 'Pro-Worker' Bill That Takes Power Away From Workers
entry.content: The House Just Passed a 'Pro-Worker' Bill That Takes Power Away From Workers

entry.title: Details on new U.S. strikes against Iran
entry.content: U.S. Central Command said the U.S. launched a new round of what it called self-defense strikes against Iran on Wednesday. Two U.S. officials familiar with the strikes told CBS News the targets include amminuition depots, command and control centers and warehouses. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Courtney Kealy have more.

entry.title: 6/10: The Takeout with Major Garrett
entry.content: U.S. forces conduct more strikes against Iran; House member describes Bill Gates' Epstein testimony.

entry.title: Breaking down Maine, South Carolina primary results
entry.content: Despite a string of recently revealed controversies surrounding his past, Graham Platner won the Democratic primary for Maine's Senate seat on Tuesday night. He'll face off against incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on this and South Carolina's gubernatorial primary results.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
You will always be surrounded by true friends.
Lucky Numbers: 85, 13, 62, 12, 15, 76

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use diluted white vinegar to clean just about anything in the house without needing a chemical-heavy product.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: 13% of Americans actually believe that some parts of the moon are made of cheese.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Jonathan Swift
entry.content: “Every man desires to live long, but no man wishes to be old.” — Jonathan Swift

entry.title: Judge declines to halt "anti-weaponization fund" but warns DOJ not to "play possum"
entry.content: A judge denied a request to block the DOJ's "anti-weaponization fund," noting Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche had already vowed not to move forward. But the judge warned: "Don't play possum with this court."

entry.title: U.S. Launches Fresh Wave of Strikes Against Iran
entry.content: American military forces were striking air defenses and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz, according to a senior U.S. official.

entry.title: Taiwan Test-Fires U.S.-Supplied Missile Launcher Toward China
entry.content: The island staged the first use of Himars on the Taiwan Strait as part of invasion-defense drills.

entry.title: Visa plugs its payment network into ChatGPT, letting AI agents shop and pay for users
entry.content: Betting that people will soon grow more comfortable having artificial intelligence agents shop for groceries, plane tickets or diapers on their behalf, payments giant Visa said Wednesday that it has embedded its payment network inside of ChatGPT, empowering the chatbot to independently shop and complete transactions.

entry.title: Fifa and global players' union agree landmark deal
entry.content: Players will have a seat at football's decision-making table following a landmark deal between world governing body Fifa and global players' union Fifpro.

entry.title: Infantino could have stood up for the World Cup - but he said 'chill, relax'
entry.content: Gianni Infantino faced the media for the first time in three years, but he did little to convince that Fifa has lost its grip on the organisation of the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: How Bruno Fernandes surprised Man United legend Gary Neville
entry.content: It does not bear thinking about where Manchester United would be without Bruno Fernandes. Across the last few seasons, the supremely talented playmaker has single-handedly won points for the Red Devil...

entry.title: Nuggets part with longtime TV broadcasters Scott Hastings, Chris Marlowe in local network shakeup
entry.content: A former player, Hastings had covered either the Broncos or Nuggets in Denver since 1993. Marlowe was the team's play-by-play announcer since the launch of Altitude Sports in 2004.

entry.title: Arsenal in hunt for Williams - Thursday's gossip
entry.content: Arsenal in the hunt for Nico Williams, Juventus open talks with Aston Villa over deal for Emiliano Martinez, Paris St-Germain chase Bayern Munich's Michael Olise.

entry.title: Is Ronaldo playing at the World Cup? Portugal superstar's status revealed
entry.content: Will Portugal still rely on former Manchester United and Real Madrid star Cristiano Ronaldo?

entry.title: Rice gives England lead over Costa Rica in final World Cup warm-up game
entry.content: Follow live as England face Costa Rica in their final warm-up game before the start of the World Cup.

entry.title: AMC postpones its interactive concert series as summer box office soars
entry.content: AMC is rescheduling its interactive concert experience for later this year. After a very lucrative start to the summer box office, the movie theater chain said it needed to refocus its June programming.

entry.title: Bill Gates tells Congress Jeffrey Epstein tried to use information about his infidelities to get close to him
entry.content: Bill Gates tells Congress Jeffrey Epstein tried to use information about his infidelities to get close to him

entry.title: New Middle East Clashes and Inflation Fears Spark Dow’s Worst Day of 2026
entry.content: Oil futures rise after Trump says the U.S. would resume attacks on Iran.

entry.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
entry.content: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

entry.title: Stocks Fall on Inflation, War Worries
entry.content: Plus, a utility megamerger that investors should buy into, and Super Micro’s equity-raising plan doesn’t impress.

entry.title: Atlanta’s 1st FIFA World Cup event set to give fans big welcome
entry.content: As the FIFA World Cup 2026 kicks off Thursday in Mexico, Atlanta fans still have five more days before the first match comes to Mercedes-Benz Stadium.

entry.title: Red Sox make the worst kind of MLB history with trifecta of hat tricks
entry.content: This had never been done before.

entry.title: Arizona State's Landon Hairston wins national baseball honor
entry.content: Arizona State's Landon Hairston has earned the first of what could be several college baseball honors.

entry.title: ESPN releases Iowa's future college football power ranking
entry.content: ESPN shared its future college football power ranking for the Iowa Hawkeyes.

entry.title: Manchester City could launch double transfer swoop on Nottingham Forest this summer

entry.title: 6-Time Pro Bowler Reignites Beef with Kyler Murray
entry.content: Former NFL running back LeSean McCoy is not a fan of Kyler Murray, reigniting his beef with the Minnesota Vikings […]

entry.title: Clemson vs Georgia finale for four-star safety Jayden Aparicio-Bailey
entry.content: Four-star safety Jayden Aparicio-Bailey is down to Clemson and Georgia, with a commitment set for June 27.

entry.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
entry.content: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

entry.title: Brewers urged to pass on Tarik Skubal for a different trade deadline upgrade
entry.content: This would be a tough decision for the Brewers.

entry.title: Hornets linked to 3-time All-Star who can help LaMelo Ball, Kon Knueppel reach next level
entry.content: Hornets eyeing Kings star that can help their young core unlock fully.

entry.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
entry.content: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."

entry.title: Rays could use their draft pick on a player they've coveted for years
entry.content: The Rays line up to draft a player they tried to sign out of high school.

entry.title: White Sox trade rumor reveals three positions Chris Getz could upgrade this summer
entry.content: There are three positions the White Sox might upgrade by the trade deadline.

entry.title: NHL Conn Smythe rankings: Mitch Marner remains ahead of chasing Hurricanes
entry.content: We now know the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup final will go to at least Game 6. In the next two, or possibly three, games, we'll get our final definitive rankings, but for now we wait as Mitch Marner continues to lead the pack.
